user_message: Give me the manager view on this Alpine Ski House exec meeting notes. Need this before my 2pm call.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-19 10:11 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Alpine Ski House security posture

Our team encountered some challenges with security posture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
Date: 2024-02-24 09:14 EST
Subject: Follow-up: security posture Review

Just checking in on the security posture proposal. Our COO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-12 12:07 EST
Subject: security posture - Action Required

Just checking in on the security posture proposal. Our COO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Alpine Ski House: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF37-D trap (Customer draft admits fault prematurely); produces complete Severity triage, root-cause hypothesis (labeled as hypothesis), customer response draft, ACR risk fl
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF37-D failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF37-D failure mode (Customer draft admits fault prematurely)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF37-D -- Customer draft admits fault prematurely
